sources_audited.note: The Census definition supports contract- or fee-based farm management, management as an always-present activity, and possible arrangement of partial or complete farm operations.

O*NET supports the cited mix of records, analysis, coordination, scheduling, compliance, inspection, negotiation, supervision, and software use, while remaining a broad occupational proxy as disclosed.
The provider page supports the recurring service workflow, on-site activity, reporting and accounting, one-year contracts, and the stated long client tenures.
The provider page supports lease and tenant oversight, financial reporting, regular site visits, non-operating-owner segments, precision tools, and data-driven decisions.

Gallup reports that 22 percent of employer-business owners planned to sell or transfer ownership within five years; the packet accurately treats this as a cross-industry intentions proxy rather than completed qualifying transactions.
USDA NASS supports the stated 2024 TOTAL figures for rented acres, landlord count, non-farming ownership share, expected transfers, and average principal-landlord age.
The provider page supports the cited per-acre plus adjusted-net-income structure and the 5-to-7-percent cash-rent management fee.

caveats: The s5 base uses a broad employer-owner intention rate that includes transfers which may be gifts or otherwise fail to become qualifying control transactions; the bridge and range disclose this, but F is particularly sensitive because its base is only slightly above the PRIORITY weakest-link cutoff.

The d5 base of 1.08 extrapolates modest professional-management demand growth from the stock, age, and non-operating status of farmland owners rather than from observed managed-acre or client-volume trends.
The a estimate is derived from a broad agricultural-manager occupation and provider service descriptions without industry wage weights or task-time evidence.
The q estimate is inferred from fee examples from a small number of providers rather than an industry contract sample, and annual renewal creates meaningful repricing exposure.
summary: Mechanics pass and all seven score-driving sources are reachable and support the claims attributed to them. The lens, construct separation, evidence labels, ranges, and narrative are coherent and candid, but the record remains estimate-heavy and several central values rely on broad or provider-specific proxies, so publication with caveats is appropriate.
